questions.shortcut: Write model, convert units, then apply formula.
questions.question: Two perpendicular electric fields of magnitudes 110 N/C and 110 N/C act at a point. Find resultant magnitude.
questions.solution: Perpendicular vectors add by Pythagoras: E_net=sqrt(E^2+E^2)=sqrt2 E=155.56 N/C.

questions.commonTrap: Forgetting direction, symmetry or SI-unit conversion.
questions.shortcut: Write model, convert units, then apply formula.
questions.question: For an infinite line charge, if distance from wire is doubled, field becomes what fraction of original?
questions.solution: E=lambda/(2pi epsilon0 r), so E is inversely proportional to r. Doubling r halves E.

questions.commonTrap: Forgetting direction, symmetry or SI-unit conversion.
questions.shortcut: Write model, convert units, then apply formula.
questions.question: For a uniformly charged solid sphere, what is E at r=R/2 in terms of surface field Es=kQ/R^2?
questions.solution: Inside E=kQr/R^3. At r=R/2, E=kQ/(2R^2)=Es/2.

questions.commonTrap: Forgetting direction, symmetry or SI-unit conversion.
questions.shortcut: Write model, convert units, then apply formula.
questions.question: A closed surface encloses charge 6epsilon0 in SI units. Find net flux.
questions.solution: Gauss law: Phi=q_enclosed/epsilon0=6epsilon0/epsilon0=6 N m^2/C.

questions.question: Why can electric field be non-zero on a closed surface even when net flux is zero?
questions.answer: Because flux is signed. Field lines entering and leaving can cancel in net flux while local E remains non-zero.
questions.solution: Because flux is signed. Field lines entering and leaving can cancel in net flux while local E remains non-zero.

questions.commonTrap: Forgetting direction, symmetry or SI-unit conversion.
questions.shortcut: Use symmetry, limiting case and vector direction.
questions.question: Why does an infinite sheet field not decrease with distance in the ideal model?
questions.answer: A larger pillbox does not enclose extra charge for fixed face area, and plane symmetry keeps E uniform on faces.
questions.solution: A larger pillbox does not enclose extra charge for fixed face area, and plane symmetry keeps E uniform on faces.

questions.question: Why does dipole field fall as 1/r^3 while point charge field falls as 1/r^2?

questions.question: Read the case and answer the subquestions.
questions.solution: Use charge sign, field direction and Gauss law conditions.
questions.caseText: A student studies a symmetric charge distribution and chooses a Gaussian surface. In trial 1, the enclosed charge is 1epsilon0 and an outside charge is moved without crossing the surface.

subquestions.question: What is the net flux through the surface?
subquestions.solution: Phi=1 N m^2/C by Gauss law.

subquestions.question: Does moving the outside charge change net flux?
subquestions.solution: No, q enclosed is unchanged.

subquestions.question: Can local electric field on the surface change?
subquestions.solution: Yes, outside charge can change local E while net flux remains same.
